structural_verification.evidence: OBViewChange retains old-session effects without re-tagging or re-broadcasting. Both OMSConsumeOB (session mismatch) and OMSViewChange (archiver query by new session) are dead paths. Permanent liveness failure.

proposal.technique_name: broadcast replication for zero-latency failover (session re-tagging on recovery)
proposal.severity: structural gap in OB-to-OMS data-plane continuity across view changes

judgment.ruling: The bug is real and the fix is correct. No material dispute between proposer and reviewer. Judgment confirms acceptance.
judgment.liveness_failure_conditions: OMS partition p is permanently stuck at oms_ob_cursor[p][m] <= last_recovered for market m after any OBViewChange where recovered > 0, unless and until OB produces brand-new effects at seq > recovered (which does not close the gap for seq <= recovered).
judgment.new_property_recommended: OBRecoveredEffectsAccessible: after OBViewChange(m) with last_recovered=r, for all seq in 1..r there exists an effect in msgs_ob_broadcast[m] with session=ob_primary[m] and that seq value.
